rationale: Gold bounced sharply from its 50% multi-year Fibonacci retracement near $3,964 and surged ~$140 on the July 3 NFP release, closing at $4,195 with RSI at 67 and MACD positive — the freshest catalyst favouring a continued short-covering squeeze toward $4,240–4,300. However, structural headwinds remain firmly intact: the Fed under Chair Warsh held at 3.50–3.75% on June 17 and markets now price a 66% probability of a hike by December, while the DXY has broken above 101 with US inflation above 4%, keeping real yields elevated and institutional targets (Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan) revised down. key_drivers: NFP miss driving short-covering rally from 3,964 Fibonacci support
Fed hawkish hold (Jun 17) with 66% Dec hike probability capping upside
DXY above 101 on inflation >4% — structural gold headwind
Warsh 'declining inflation expectations' comment — small dovish wrinkle
Central bank buying providing a structural floor through the correction
Key supply zone 4,240–4,300 limits near-term upside
